name: 01 - Realistic Easy Suite
description: Real-data business questions at the easy level — single table or one lookup. Every scenario has ExpectedSql so the runner uses Execution Accuracy (result-set comparison).

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets do we have in total?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Total FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many open tickets do we have right now?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS OpenTickets FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Open' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show me all unassigned tickets.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.AssignedToUserId IS NULL AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets were created today?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Today FROM Tickets WHERE CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) = CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE) AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets were created yesterday?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Yesterday FROM Tickets WHERE CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) = CAST(DATEADD(day, -1, GETDATE()) AS DATE) AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many closed tickets do we have?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Closed FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Closed' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets does each priority have?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT p.Name AS Priority, COUNT(*) AS Total FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY p.Name ORDER BY Total DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show me all critical tickets.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE p.Name = 'Critical' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many active users do we have?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS ActiveUsers FROM AspNetUsers WHERE IsActive = 1

Scenarios.Question: How many active entities do we have?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS ActiveEntities FROM Entitys WHERE IsActive = 1

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets came in through email?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS EmailTickets FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketSources s ON s.Id = t.SourceId WHERE s.Name = 'Email' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show me the 10 newest tickets.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 10 t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets were resolved this month?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS ResolvedThisMonth FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND YEAR(ResolvedAt) = YEAR(GETDATE()) AND MONTH(ResolvedAt) = MONTH(GETDATE()) AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets have their SLA breached?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Breached FROM Tickets WHERE IsSlaBreached = 1 AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show me tickets created in the last 7 days.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt >= DATEADD(day, -7, GETDATE()) AND t.IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets whose title contains 'login'.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE '%login%' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many rejected tickets are there?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Rejected FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Rejected' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets are currently still open (not closed yet)?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS StillOpen FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.IsClosedState = 0 AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets have been escalated?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Escalated FROM Tickets WHERE EscalatedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many comments are there across all tickets?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS TotalComments FROM TicketComments
